headline: USAR SURGES 13% ON $2.8 BILLION DEAL — Serra Verde Makes USA Rare Earth the Only Non-Asian Supplier of All 4 Magnetic Rare Earths
snippet: USA Rare Earth (NASDAQ: USAR) agreed to acquire Brazil's Serra Verde Group for US$2.8 billion — funded with $300M cash and 126.8M newly issued shares — creating the only mine-to-magnet rare earth supply chain outside Asia. The stock closed April 20 at $22.58, up 13.18%, on volume 118% above its three-month average. Deal expected to close Q3 2026.

headline: GOLD RETREATS TO $4,780 as Iran Ceasefire Nears Expiry — Two-Week Truce Ends This Week, Second Round of Talks Uncertain
snippet: Gold fell to $4,782 on April 21, down 0.81% on the session, as investors braced for expiry of the US-Iran two-week ceasefire with no guarantee of a follow-on deal. Despite the near-term pressure, gold is up 43% year-over-year and 8.5% over the past month, with JPMorgan targeting $5,055 by Q4 and Deutsche Bank forecasting $6,000 by year-end.

headline: IMPACT SILVER DRILLS 1,295 g/t Ag AND 114.5 g/t Au in 61cm Core — Carlos Pacheco Vein Extension Adds Copper Kicker at Noche Buena
snippet: Impact Silver Corp. (TSXV: IPT) reported 114.5 g/t gold and 1,295 g/t silver over 0.61m within a broader 9.79 g/t Au and 212 g/t Ag interval spanning 9.49m on the Carlos Pacheco Vein extension at its producing Noche Buena mine in Mexico. The intersection is 150m north of active workings and also carries 1.10% copper — a polymetallic angle the company says may warrant separate mining consideration.

headline: REVIVAL GOLD EXPANDS DRILL PROGRAM After Hitting 6.4 g/t Au Over 19m in Idaho Step-Out — Two Rigs Now Running at Beartrack-Arnett
snippet: Revival Gold (TSXV: RVG; OTCQX: RVLGF) drilled 5.4 g/t gold over 32.6m including 6.4 g/t over 19.1m at 573m depth — a 100-metre step-out to the south at Beartrack-Arnett in Idaho. The result was strong enough to immediately expand the current program to 4,900m with a second rig. Two additional holes confirmed the target shear zone with assays pending.

headline: U.S. GOLDMINING BOOTS UP Whistler, Alaska Drill Program — District-Scale Gold-Copper System Has Two Undrilled Deposits Sitting Idle
snippet: U.S. GoldMining Inc. has mobilized its 2026 diamond core drill program at the Whistler Gold-Copper Project in Alaska, targeting high-priority near-deposit extensions and new satellite targets in the Whistler-Raintree corridor. The company's existing PEA models only one of three deposits with defined mineral resources — leaving two entirely undrilled and the district-scale thesis untested.

headline: GOLD-SILVER RATIO CONTRACTING HARD — Historically Bullish Signal as Silver Outperforms Gold for Second Straight Week
snippet: Silver surged 6.2% in the past week, outpacing gold and compressing the gold-to-silver ratio sharply from its April peak above 105. GoldSeek's weekly SWOT analysis flags ratio contraction as a historically reliable setup for a sustained silver catch-up rally — with the long-run average near 60 implying significant room to run if the trend holds.

headline: TSX-V SCOREBOARD: Tincorp Metals +128% on 697koz Gold-Copper Resource, Q-Gold Resources +103% After Maiden PEA
snippet: This week's top TSX Venture performers were led by Tincorp Metals (+128%), whose Santa Barbara project hosts an indicated 697,000 oz gold and 68.2 million lb copper, and Q-Gold Resources (+103%) on a maiden preliminary economic assessment for its Quartz Mountain project. Context: British Columbia's mineral exploration sector hit a record C$751 million in spend in 2025, up 36% from the prior year.
